channel.title: The ParrotTalk Blog
channel.description: A founder's journal about building ParrotTalk, a 100% free IELTS practice site with AI feedback.

item.title: Making sure we grade what you wrote
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: The honest story of a bug that let the graded essay differ from the one on screen, how I fixed it, and why integrity is the product.

item.title: Never lose an essay again
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Your essay now saves as you type, so a stray tab close or a reload never wipes your work. A small seatbelt, big peace of mind.

item.title: The surprise in my notes: assistants were sending me students
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: A lovely early surprise: AI assistants began recommending ParrotTalk on their own, and why honesty is what made it findable.

item.title: Giving each speaker a different voice
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Rebuilding the Listening audio so each section has its own distinct voice, the way exam day actually sounds.

item.title: Putting the exam clock on Listening
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Adding a single running clock and automatic submission to Listening, so a free practice test feels like the real computer-delivered exam.

item.title: When someone skips a question
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Real people do not answer in a tidy order. The small saga of making Speaking practice survive skipped questions and messy input.

item.title: Free IELTS practice for candidates in Vietnam, Bangladesh, Pakistan and Uzbekistan
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Full IELTS practice for all four skills, 100% free, and built to be genuinely usable from Hanoi to Tashkent.

item.title: An AI checker for IELTS Writing Task 2: what it looks at
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: How ParrotTalk reads a Writing Task 2 essay: a band on each of the four official criteria, and feedback you can act on.

item.title: What a free IELTS mock test really means
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: What a full IELTS mock test actually means: all four sections, back to back, under real exam timing, and completely free.

item.title: Moving the AI off the browser
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: The first real backend decision: moving the AI evaluation off the browser and behind a small server of our own.

item.title: Opening the doors, and keeping them open
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: The day ParrotTalk went from a private project to an open public beta, what real users taught me, and the honest plan for measuring the scoring.

item.title: Rewriting the FAQ around proof, not promises
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Rebuilding the FAQ from the actual code rather than from what I wished were true, and admitting a real gap.

item.title: Starting to measure whether any of this works
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Installing measurement early, looking at genuinely tiny numbers, and refusing to tell myself a flattering story about them.

item.title: The day a passing test lied to me
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: My test suite was green. The site was broken. The day a passing test told me a comfortable lie.

item.title: The bug that mattered most: never losing your progress
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: The most important fix of the whole project was not a feature. It was making sure a reload never costs you your answers.

item.title: The honesty pass: cutting every claim I could not stand behind
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Early on I went through every page of ParrotTalk and removed anything that was not strictly true. The day it stopped being a demo.

item.title: Ten bugs, and letting you submit one section at a time
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: An unglamorous bug-fix day, and the feature hiding inside the bug list: practice one IELTS section at a time.

item.title: Teaching a parrot to grade a voice in one day
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: Day one of ParrotTalk: building browser voice recording and AI Speaking feedback in a single day.

item.title: How our AI scoring works, and how accurate it is
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: How ParrotTalk estimates your IELTS band with AI, what we calibrated to make it fair, and the limits we are honest about.

item.title: I couldn't find an IELTS site I liked, so I built one by talking to an AI
item.author: contact@parrottalk.app (Xavier, founder of ParrotTalk)
item.description: The honest story of building ParrotTalk, a 100% free IELTS practice site with AI feedback. From a one-day prototype to a real platform, one fix at a time.
